results.question: What is the refund policy for HelixDB paid plans?
results.answer: HelixDB offers a 30-day money-back guarantee for first-time subscribers on Starter and Growth plans [chunk_1]. To request a refund, users must contact billing@helixdb.io within the first 30 days with their account email and reason for cancellation [chunk_1]. After 30 days, no prorated refunds are offered for unused subscription time, and Enterprise plan terms may vary [chunk_1].

results.question: How does API key authentication work in HelixDB?
results.answer: HelixDB issues a primary API key upon registration, and additional keys can be created via the dashboard with specific names, expiry dates, and IP allowlists [chunk_1]. Authentication is performed by passing the key in the `Authorization` header using the format `Authorization: Bearer [key]` [chunk_1]. Each key is assigned one of three permission scopes: Read-only for queries, Write for data ingestion and retention management, or Admin for full access including user management and billing [chunk_1].

results.question: What are the minimum system requirements for the HelixDB Community Edition?
results.answer: The minimum system requirements for the HelixDB Community Edition include a Linux operating system (Ubuntu 20.04+, Debian 11+, RHEL 8+, or any Linux with kernel 5.4+), at least 4 CPU cores, and 8 GB of RAM [chunk_1]. Additionally, the system requires an SSD for storage and a minimum network speed of 1 Gbps [chunk_1].

results.question: How do I reset my HelixDB account password?
results.answer: To reset your HelixDB account password, navigate to https://app.helixdb.io/forgot-password and enter your registered email address [chunk_1]. You will receive a reset link via email that remains valid for one hour; click this link to enter your new password [chunk_1]. Once the reset is complete, all existing sessions for your account are invalidated [chunk_1].

results.question: How much does the HelixDB Growth Plan cost and what does it include?
results.answer: The HelixDB Growth Plan costs $199 per month [chunk_1]. It includes up to 50 million data points per day, 1-year data retention, unlimited dashboard workspaces, and all export formats including CSV, JSON, Parquet, and Arrow [chunk_1]. Additionally, the plan provides priority email support with an 8-hour response SLA, REST API and Python/JavaScript SDKs, and webhook integrations [chunk_1].

results.question: How do I deploy HelixDB using Docker?
results.answer: To deploy HelixDB using Docker, first pull the latest community image with the command `docker pull helixdb/community:latest` [chunk_1]. You can then start the container by running `docker run -d -p 8086:8086 -v /data/helix:/var/lib/helixdb helixdb/community:latest` [chunk_1]. This command runs the database in detached mode, maps port 8086, and mounts a local data volume to the container [chunk_1].
